title: Healthcare AI Regulation
content: The FDA has established a regulatory framework for AI/ML-based Software as a Medical Device (SaMD). This framework requires continuous monitoring of AI system performance and mandates that changes to AI algorithms go through a predetermined change control plan. Healthcare AI systems must demonstrate clinical validity and maintain transparency in their decision-making processes.

title: Financial AI Compliance
content: Financial institutions using AI for credit scoring must comply with the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA) and Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA). These regulations require explainability in automated lending decisions and prohibit discrimination based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, or age. Model risk management guidelines from the OCC require thorough validation and ongoing monitoring.

title: Bias in Facial Recognition
content: Studies by MIT and NIST have shown that facial recognition systems exhibit significant accuracy disparities across demographic groups. Error rates for dark-skinned females can be up to 34% higher than for light-skinned males. This has led to several municipalities banning government use of facial recognition technology and calls for stronger regulatory oversight.
